# Agentic Flow v2.5.3 - run log

**Date:** 2026-05-01
**Branch:** `v2.5.3`
**Scope:** Replace multi-slice coder batches with single-slice `slice-run` execution and add dedicated E2E ownership before the next real delivery run
**Compared to:** `v2.5.2`

---

## Version Updates

### Change Summary

| Area | How it was | What is changed | What is now |
|------|------------|-----------------|-------------|
| Implementation unit | `flow-log`, prompts, and docs still treated multi-slice `batch` as the active coder unit | Replaced batch state, commands, summaries, and event names with `slice-run` equivalents | One coder invocation now maps to exactly one approved slice and one owned changed-file list |
| Verification vocabulary | Intermediate verification still used `verify --profile batch` even though the next iteration was supposed to stop thinking in batches | Renamed the intermediate verification profile to `slice` across CLI help, tests, prompts, and quality instructions | Verification language now matches the one-slice execution model |
| TL / Coder handoff | TL and Coder instructions still described active slice arrays and batch-owned file lists | Rewired prompts to `summary.sliceRuns.current.slice`, `summary.sliceRuns.current.type`, `summary.sliceRuns.current.changedFiles`, and explicit `intermediate` / `final` slice-run handoff type | The next run should start from one active slice with a state-backed handoff type, not from grouped coder work |
| Workflow docs | Overview, log template, and README still documented `start-batch`, `complete-batch`, and per-batch wording | Updated active docs to `start-slice-run`, `complete-slice-run`, and sequential slice-run flow | The active written workflow now matches the tested CLI surface |
| Legacy implementation surface | Old `batches.mjs` modules still existed in the repo after the command cut | Removed the obsolete batch modules after the slice-run tests passed | No dead internal path remains for the old abstraction |
| Smoke ownership | Architect still owned Karate authoring, Coder still owned final `karate` execution, and no role owned repeatability end to end | Added a dedicated `E2E Tester` role, a pre-plan E2E scenario artifact, separate E2E instructions, and post-implementation `karate` ownership | One role now owns scenario design, repeatable Karate updates, and the smoke verdict |
| Shared-state artifact tracking | flow-log tracked only story and plan approvals, so the new E2E pass would otherwise live only in prompts | Added approval-tracked `e2e` artifacts to flow-log summary/status/readiness and documented the new artifact path | E2E coverage intent is now on disk and visible in the canonical shared state |
| Conditional E2E invocation | The first cut made E2E unconditional, which was too expensive for internal refactors and business-logic-only changes | Added explicit `set-e2e-mode --mode <REUSE_EXISTING|SCENARIOS_REQUIRED>` state plus prompt/doc rules for when E2E Tester is actually needed | API/smoke-coverage changes invoke E2E Tester; non-API changes reuse existing Karate and Team Lead runs the smoke check directly |
| Failure handling | flow-log kept only short `outputTail` snippets, so diagnosing failed checks required re-running commands or reopening raw terminals | Added persisted redacted check logs plus a `check-log` command for retrieving the latest stored log tail for a check | Failed verification now leaves durable, queryable evidence without leaking raw secret-bearing output into artifacts |
| Failure-handling adoption | The first `check-log` cut improved the tool surface, but active prompts still mostly described only `outputTail` and manual retries | Updated Team Lead, Java Coder, E2E Tester, Code Reviewer, and shared verification instructions to call `check-log` when checks fail, block, or look suspect | Failure retrieval is now part of the active workflow instead of a hidden optional command |
| Explicit review debt | Medium/Low risks and findings could pass through the workflow while still looking `OPEN`, which hid whether the debt was intentional or forgotten | Added `decide-risk` / `decide-finding`, new `ACCEPTED` / `DEFERRED` states, debt metadata in summary/gates, and signoff blocking for undecided non-blocking review items | Non-blocking architecture and code-review debt is now explicit, queryable, and must be intentionally recorded before final acceptance |

### Addressed Findings From Previous Log Analysis

| Issue spotted | How was addressed | What pain point should be solved |
|---------------|-------------------|----------------------------------|
| Multi-slice execution unit stayed too broad and failure-prone | Added `start-slice-run` / `complete-slice-run`, `sliceRuns` summary state, single-slice changed-file ownership, and single-slice TL/Coder handoff rules | Reduces coder context load and keeps failure recovery local to one approved slice |
| Batch vocabulary leaked into verification and kept the old mental model alive | Renamed the intermediate verify profile from `batch` to `slice` and updated the active instructions together with the CLI | Stops the workflow from silently carrying the old abstraction under new wording |
| TL handoff still allowed grouped coder work | TL prompt and `flow-log` now require one approved slice per invocation plus a state-backed `intermediate` vs `final` slice-run type | Makes the next run’s coder contract unambiguous |
| Prompt / doc drift was likely after a tooling-only cut | Updated overview, log template, README, active agent prompts, and local verification instructions in the same increment | Reduces contradictory instructions before the next real run |
| Missing E2E owner around Karate design, repeatability, and smoke verdict | Added a dedicated `E2E Tester` agent, approval-tracked `artifacts/e2e-scenarios/<feature>.e2e.md`, separate E2E/Karate instructions, and a smoke pass that owns `run-check --name karate` | Removes the biggest remaining cross-agent ownership gap from `v2.5.2` |
| E2E was over-applied to non-API changes | Added an explicit E2E mode decision. `SCENARIOS_REQUIRED` is used for API/smoke-coverage changes; `REUSE_EXISTING` skips E2E authoring and keeps the existing Karate run with Team Lead | Avoids paying an extra agent cost when no smoke authoring work is needed |
| Failed checks were hard to inspect after the fact | Persisted redacted logs for `run-check` / `verify`, stored the latest log path on the check entry, and added `check-log --name <check>` for retrieval | Makes failure evidence durable and queryable without widening `status` or `summary` |
| Non-blocking review items looked unresolved forever | Added explicit TL debt decisions (`ACCEPTED` / `DEFERRED`) for Medium/Low risks and findings, wired prompts/docs to use them, and blocked signoff on undecided non-blocking debt | Makes intentional delivery debt explicit instead of hiding it in lingering `OPEN` items |

### Current Alignment Snapshot (2026-05-02)

- Implemented: `flow-log` command surface now uses `start-slice-run --type <intermediate|final>`, `complete-slice-run`, and `verify --profile slice`.
- Implemented: `summary.sliceRuns.current` now carries one active slice ID and one slice-run-owned changed-file list.
- Implemented: `summary.sliceRuns.current.type` now carries the durable `intermediate` / `final` handoff decision.
- Implemented: `status` now reports `currentSliceRun`, `currentSlice`, `currentSliceRunType`, and `totalSliceRuns`.
- Implemented: Team Lead and Java Coder prompts now enforce one approved slice per coder invocation.
- Implemented: flow-log now approval-tracks `e2e` artifacts and exposes `summary.artifacts.e2e`, `status.e2eApproved`, and `status.e2eStale`.
- Implemented: flow-log now records `set-e2e-mode --mode <REUSE_EXISTING|SCENARIOS_REQUIRED>` and exposes the decision in summary/status/readiness.
- Implemented: Team Lead now records an explicit E2E mode and routes `E2E Tester` only when API-facing behavior or smoke coverage changes require dedicated scenario or Karate updates.
- Implemented after conditionality fix: `E2E Tester` is now invoked only when API-facing behavior or smoke coverage changes require scenario or Karate updates. For internal refactors and business-logic-only changes, Team Lead reuses the existing Karate suite and runs `karate` directly.
- Implemented: `run-check` and `verify` now persist redacted check logs under `artifacts/check-logs/<feature>/...` and store the latest `logPath` on each check entry.
- Implemented: `check-log --feature <name> --name <verifyQuick|finalCheck|karate> [--lines <n>]` now returns the latest persisted log tail for the selected check.
- Implemented: Team Lead, Java Coder, E2E Tester, Code Reviewer, and shared verification instructions now use `check-log` as the standard follow-up when verification fails, blocks, or needs deeper inspection.
- Implemented: `decide-risk` and `decide-finding` now record explicit `ACCEPTED` / `DEFERRED` debt decisions for non-blocking review items, including reason and optional follow-up reference.
- Implemented: architecture/code-review summaries and gates now surface accepted, deferred, and undecided non-blocking debt counts; signoff blocks until non-blocking debt is explicitly decided.
- Implemented: Java Coder now stops at `verify --profile slice`; `coder-handoff-check.sh` no longer requires `karate` for coder handoff.
- Implemented: when `e2eMode=SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`, `E2E Tester` now owns the approved scenario artifact, Karate `.feature` / runner updates, repeatability rules, and `run-check --name karate`.
- Implemented: new E2E support artifacts landed: `.github/agents/e2e-tester.agent.md`, `.github/instructions/e2e-tester-karate.instructions.md`, and `artifacts/templates/e2e-scenarios.example.md`.
- Implemented: legacy batch modules were removed after the slice-run control-plane tests passed.

### Deferred From v2.5.2 Analysis

- Deferred: Java Coder model change to GPT-5.4. The slice-run cut should be exercised first so the next model test has cleaner signal.

## Flow Setup

This is the active `v2.5.3` flow as implemented in the repository before the next run.

### Planning Phase Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        INTENDED FLOW                                 │
│                                                                      │
│ 1:TL ─► 2:PM ─► 3:TL ─► 4:TL ─► 5:ARCH ─► 6:TL                  │
│ lock      story     story-gate  e2e-mode   plan       plan-gate    │
│                                                                      │
│ if `SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`: 4:TL ─► E2E ─► TL before planning          │
│                                                                      │
│   6:TL ──► 7:ARCH-REV ◄──► 5:ARCH (scoped loop) ──► 8:TL           │
│    increment     risks (UNCLASSIFIED)          arch-gate PASS        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Implementation Phase Diagram

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                                      │
│   8:TL ─────────────────────► 9:CODER ──────────────────► 10:TL    │
│    start-slice-run (1 slice+type) verify + add-change                │
│                                                                      │
│   10:TL ──► 8:TL (next slice if needed)                             │
│                                                                      │
│ if `SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`: 10:TL ─► E2E ─► 11:TL                     │
│ if `REUSE_EXISTING`: 10:TL ─► TL karate run-check ─► 11:TL         │
│                                                                      │
│  11:TL ──► 12:CODE-REV ◄──► 9:CODER ─► smoke owner ─► 13:TL       │
│   increment   findings         fix loop      if karate stale signoff │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Flow Step Clarification

- **1:TL** — Requirement lock; `create` + `lock-requirements` with request source path
- **2:PM** — Produces `artifacts/user-stories/<feature>.story.md`; story must preserve locked scope and acceptance criteria and seed `External Contracts` when boundary contracts matter
- **3:TL** — Story gate; `register-artifact story` + `approve-artifact story`
- **4:TL** — Records `set-e2e-mode --mode <REUSE_EXISTING|SCENARIOS_REQUIRED>` based on the story and expected smoke impact
- **4:E2E** — Only when `SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`: produces `artifacts/e2e-scenarios/<feature>.e2e.md` from the locked request and approved story; captures stable scenario IDs, smoke coverage, and repeatability rules
- **4:TL** — Only when `SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`: E2E gate; `register-artifact e2e` + `approve-artifact e2e`
- **5:ARCH** — Produces `artifacts/implementation-plans/<feature>.plan.json` via the v4 draft lifecycle; plan remains slice-first, external payload details stay in story `External Contracts`, and smoke intent stays in the approved E2E artifact when that artifact exists
- **6:TL** — Plan gate; `validate-plan` + `plan-summary` + `register-artifact plan` + `approve-artifact plan`; then `increment-round` and invoke Architecture Reviewer
- **7:ARCH-REV** — Records risks via `add-risk` (all UNCLASSIFIED); TL classifies severity via `reclassify-risk`; TL runs `architecture-gate`
- **8:TL** — Architecture gate passed; `set-review architectureReview PASS`; starts one approved slice-run at a time via `start-slice-run --slice <id> --type <intermediate|final>`
- **9:CODER** — Reads `summary`, then `summary.sliceRuns.current.slice`, `summary.sliceRuns.current.type`, `summary.sliceRuns.current.changedFiles`, then slice-specific plan data; runs `verify --profile slice` for each slice-run and records files via `add-change`
- **10:TL** — Checks `status` and `summary`; validates story / plan staleness, active slice-run state, the recorded `e2eMode`, and slice-run-owned changed files; re-runs only stale or suspect checks; completes the slice-run or starts the next approved one; selects the smoke owner after the final slice-run
- **11:SMOKE OWNER** — If `SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`, `E2E Tester` updates Karate `.feature` files and runners from the approved E2E artifact, records changed files via `add-change`, and runs `run-check --name karate`. If `REUSE_EXISTING`, Team Lead runs `run-check --name karate` directly against the existing suite.
- **12:CODE-REV** — Records findings via `add-finding`; updates docs and `.http` examples as the interim documentation owner
- **13:TL** — Final audit gate; `readiness signoff` must return `ready: true`, and stale story / plan approvals plus stale `finalCheck` / `karate` remain blocking; stale E2E approvals block only when `e2eMode=SCENARIOS_REQUIRED`

## Pre-Run Validation

Validated after the `slice-run`, conditional E2E, failure-handling, and explicit review-debt cuts:

- `cd flow-log && npm test`
- Result: **70 passing, 0 failing**

This validation covered the renamed command surface, slice-run lifecycle and type, explicit `e2eMode` decision handling, changed-file ownership, conditional E2E artifact approval state in summary/status/readiness, persisted redacted check logs plus `check-log` retrieval, explicit `ACCEPTED` / `DEFERRED` review-debt decisions and signoff blocking for undecided non-blocking debt, coder handoff no longer requiring `karate`, and unchanged plan/query behavior.

## User Suggestions (manually populated by user)

- Add a dedicated `E2E Tester` agent to own repeatable Karate / smoke coverage end to end. (Implemented in current repo.)
- Improve `flow-log` failure accessibility before moving shared state behind MCP. (Implemented in current repo.)
- Add explicit `ACCEPTED` / `DEFERRED` states for non-blocking risks and findings. (Implemented in current repo.)
- Consider moving `Java Coder` to GPT-5.4 after the first real slice-run trial.
